title: Agora Phase 1: Core Library Skeleton
description: Implement the complete Phase 1 of Agora: the core Python library that lets an operator subclass AgoraBot, implement should_respond/generate_response, point at a YAML config, and have a working Discord bot.
design:
## Implementation Approach

Phase 1 delivers the core Agora library skeleton: package structure, config loading, message wrapper, chunker, AgoraBot base class, CLI scaffolding, example agents, and full test suite.

### Architecture
- **Config (config.py)**: YAML-loaded dataclass with token_env, channels, exchange_cap, rate_limit, jitter, response behavior. Validates on load.
- **Message (message.py)**: Thin wrapper over discord.Message exposing content, author_name, author_id, is_bot, is_mention, channel_name, channel_id, id, reference_id. Decouples operator code from discord.py.
- **Chunker (chunker.py)**: Splits >2000 char responses at paragraph/newline/space/hard boundaries. Preserves code blocks.
- **AgoraBot (bot.py)**: Base class with should_respond/generate_response dispatch pipeline. Handles self-filtering, channel mode enforcement, jitter, typing indicator, chunking, reply threading. from_config classmethod for subclass-safe construction.
- **CLI (cli.py)**: agora init <name> scaffolding command generating agent.py, agent.yaml, run.sh templates.

### Acceptance Criteria
- pip install from local path works
- Subclassing AgoraBot with two method overrides produces a functional Discord bot
- All unit tests pass without Discord connection
- Live integration tests pass with real Discord bot
- Echo and keyword example agents work end-to-end

### File Changes
Creates: agora/__init__.py, agora/bot.py, agora/config.py, agora/message.py, agora/chunker.py, agora/cli.py, pyproject.toml, agent.yaml, examples/echo_agent.py, examples/keyword_agent.py, tests/test_config.py, tests/test_chunker.py, tests/test_message.py, tests/test_bot_unit.py, tests/test_cli.py, tests/integration/test_live_bot.py

title: Package scaffold + Config module
description: Create the package structure and Config module. Files: pyproject.toml (metadata, deps: discord.py>=2.7,<3 + pyyaml>=6,<7, dev deps: pytest>=8 + pytest-asyncio>=0.23, console_scripts entry point), agora/__init__.py (version, public exports: AgoraBot, Message, Config), agora/config.py (Config and RateLimitConfig dataclasses, from_yaml classmethod, token property reading env var, validation: token_env required, channel modes must be subscribe/mention-only/write-only, exchange_cap>=1, jitter min<=max, max_response_length>=1), agent.yaml (example config at repo root). Tests: tests/test_config.py covering valid load, defaults, missing token_env, invalid channel mode, invalid exchange_cap, invalid jitter, token property reads env var, token property raises when env var unset.

title: Message wrapper + Chunker
description: Create Message wrapper and chunk_message function. Files: agora/message.py (Message class wrapping discord.Message with properties: content, author_name, author_id, is_bot, is_mention, channel_name, channel_id, id, reference_id. Constructor takes discord_message and bot_user_id. Does NOT expose raw discord.Message), agora/chunker.py (chunk_message function: splits at paragraph boundary > newline > space > hard split. Preserves code blocks across chunks. Returns [''] for empty input. Each chunk <= max_length, stripped). Tests: tests/test_message.py (all properties from mock discord.Message, is_mention detection, is_bot flag, edge cases: empty content, no reference), tests/test_chunker.py (short msg single chunk, exactly 2000 single chunk, 2001 two chunks, paragraph split, newline split, space split, hard split, code block preservation, empty string, unicode).

title: AgoraBot core dispatch pipeline
description: Create the AgoraBot base class — the heart of the library. File: agora/bot.py. AgoraBot wraps discord.Client with intents (message_content=True, members=False). Internal state: _channel_map, _channel_ids. Event handlers: _on_ready (resolve channels, check intents), _on_message (full 10-step dispatch pipeline: ignore self, check channel config, build Message wrapper, enforce mention-only mode, call should_respond, jitter delay, typing indicator, call generate_response, truncate+chunk, send with reply threading). Public API: should_respond(message)->bool (default: is_mention), generate_response(message)->str|None (default: None), from_config(path)->Self (classmethod, subclass-safe), run() (blocking). Exception handling: should_respond/generate_response errors are logged not propagated. Helpers: _resolve_channels, _get_channel_mode (empty config = mention-only everywhere), _check_intents. Tests: tests/test_bot_unit.py with mocked discord.py covering: ignores own messages, ignores unconfigured channels, ignores write-only, ignores non-mention in mention-only, calls should_respond for subscribe, calls generate_response when should_respond=True, skips generate_response when should_respond=False, sends via reply/send based on threading config, chunks long responses, swallows exceptions from both methods, empty channels config = mention-only, from_config returns correct subclass.

title: CLI scaffolding + Example agents
description: Create the agora init CLI command and example agents. Files: agora/cli.py (init_agent function creating <name>/ directory with agent.py template containing AgoraBot subclass stubs, agent.yaml template with token_env placeholder, run.sh template. main() entry point parsing args. Registered as console_scripts in pyproject.toml), examples/echo_agent.py (simplest agent — echoes back any message it's mentioned in), examples/keyword_agent.py (responds when configurable keywords detected, no LLM). Tests: tests/test_cli.py (init creates directory with expected files, agent.py compiles, agent.yaml loads via Config.from_yaml, run.sh is executable, raises if dir exists, handles special chars in name).

title: Human integration testing with live Discord bot
description: Live integration testing with a real Discord server. REQUIRES HUMAN SETUP: Human creates Discord server, creates bot application at discord.com/developers, enables MESSAGE_CONTENT intent, invites bot to server with Send Messages + Read Message History permissions, creates #test channel, provides TEST_BOT_TOKEN. AGENT SETUP: Configure echo_agent pointing at #test channel, set up agent.yaml with the test token, start the bot process on the server. TEST PLAN (tests/integration/test_live_bot.py with --live flag): Test 1 - Connection (bot online within 10s, log shows Connected), Test 2 - Mention response (reply within jitter_max+5s, correct content, threaded), Test 3 - Non-mention ignored, Test 4 - Write-only channel respected, Test 5 - Long message chunking (5000 chars -> 3 messages), Test 6 - Typing indicator fires before response, Test 7 - Graceful error handling (should_respond raises, bot survives). Human verifies bot behavior in Discord UI.

title: Post-testing revisions and polish
description: After human integration testing, address any issues found during live testing. Fix bugs discovered during Discord testing, adjust behavior based on human feedback (timing, chunking edge cases, error messages, logging output), ensure all unit tests still pass after fixes, update example agents if needed, verify the full operator workflow end-to-end: pip install from local path -> agora init my-agent -> edit agent.py -> run -> bot works on Discord. This is the final quality gate before Phase 1 is considered complete.
